findings.evidence: TauCeti line 38 calls `ncard_primesOver_mul_ramificationIdxIn_mul_inertiaDegIn P B G`; Mathlib declares it with `[IsDomain A] ... [IsDomain B] ... [Flat A B]` at `Mathlib/NumberTheory/RamificationInertia/Galois.lean:216-222`, and `IsDedekindDomain` plus `IsTorsionFree` is only a way to obtain `Flat` at `Mathlib/RingTheory/Flat/TorsionFree.lean:143-145`.
findings.fix: Restate the theorem with `[IsDomain A] [IsDomain B] [Module.Finite A B] [Flat A B]` and remove the Dedekind/torsion-free assumptions; Dedekind-domain uses will recover `Flat` from the existing torsion-free instance.
findings.issue: `ncard_primesOver_eq_natCard_iff_of_isGaloisGroup` requires `[IsDedekindDomain A]`, `[IsDedekindDomain B]`, and `[IsTorsionFree A B]`, but the proof only uses Mathlib's fundamental identity under `[IsDomain A]`, `[IsDomain B]`, and `[Flat A B]`.

summary: The prime/maximal generalization is fixed, but the public Dedekind wrapper is still stated with stronger hypotheses than the Mathlib theorem it packages. It should expose the domain/flat level now used upstream.
